module1.title: Linux Navigation
module1.story: Orientation day. IT dropped you into a workstation with a maze of folders and no map. Before they'll grant you real access, you need to prove you can find your own way around.
module1.mission: Locate the onboarding key hidden somewhere inside your home directory.

objectives.label: Print your current directory with pwd
List directory contents with ls
Navigate into records/logs/archive with cd
Read the hidden key file with cat

hints: pwd tells you where you are right now — start there.
ls -la will reveal hidden files too, not just the obvious ones.
The key lives at records/logs/archive/.keyfile — cd there and cat it.

module2.title: File Operations
module2.story: The workstation from Module 1 is now yours, but it's a mess. Your supervisor wants the inbox cleaned up: organize what matters, discard what doesn't.
module2.mission: Create a backup folder, copy the manifest into it, move the draft into its final form, and delete the junk file.

objectives.label: Create workspace/backup with mkdir
Copy inbox/manifest.txt into workspace/backup
Move inbox/draft.txt to workspace/final.txt
Remove workspace/junk.tmp with rm

hints: mkdir -p can create the whole workspace/backup path in one shot.
cp inbox/manifest.txt workspace/backup/ keeps the original in place.
mv both renames and relocates — use it to turn draft.txt into workspace/final.txt.
The key is hidden in a dotfile: ls -a in module2/.vault

module3.title: Searching & Filtering
module3.story: Someone on the team logged an access key deep inside a pile of rotated log files and now nobody remembers which one. Your job: find it without opening every file by hand.
module3.mission: Use find, grep, and head/tail to locate the log line containing the access key.

objectives.label: Use find to search the data directory
Use grep to filter file contents
Use head or tail to inspect a file
Locate the line containing ACCESS_KEY

hints: find /home/student/module3/data -type f lists every log file, however deep.
grep -r 'ACCESS_KEY' /home/student/module3/data searches recursively in one command.
Once you've narrowed it down, tail the specific file to read the last lines.

module4.title: Permissions & Shell Scripting
module4.story: A deployment script has been sitting broken for weeks because nobody fixed its permissions or ran it as the right user. Fix it and let it do its job.
module4.mission: Make run.sh executable, fix report.txt's ownership, and run the script to unlock the key.

objectives.label: Make scripts/run.sh executable with chmod
Use sudo at least once
Change the owner of data/report.txt with chown
Execute run.sh so it unlocks the key

hints: sudo chown student data/report.txt changes the owner (you'll need sudo).
Run the script with ./run.sh once it's executable — check what file it creates.

module5.title: Advanced Linux
module5.story: Capstone day. A misbehaving background process is eating resources, and the only way to identify and stop it — then extract what it left behind — is by combining everything you've learned so far.
module5.mission: Find the runaway process, kill it, then pipe and redirect your way to the final key.

objectives.label: Use ps or top to inspect running processes
Use kill to stop the runaway process
Chain commands together with a pipe (|)
Use awk or sed with output redirection

hints: ps aux will show you the runaway sleep process and its PID.
kill <pid> stops it — the PID is also saved in module5/.runaway_pid.
Try: cat .final/key.txt | awk '{print $1}' — combine pipes and redirection to explore.
